modules.module_title: Introduction to Martial Arts, Self-Defence Concepts & Situational Awareness
Stand-up Striking Mechanics: Punches, Kicks, Elbows & Knees
Grappling, Clenches, Escapes & Defending Common Attacks
De-escalation Techniques, Legal Boundaries & Verbal Self-Defence
Safety, Sparring Supervision, Injury Prevention & First Aid
Professional Ethics, Class Management & Building Dojang/Dojo Brand

questions.question: Martial Arts – Self Defence Trainer ka primary role kya hai?

options.A: Students ko striking, grappling, situational awareness aur safe self-defence techniques seekhana
options.B: Only encouraging students to start street fights for practice
options.C: Operating heavy weapons and artillery equipment
options.D: Zero class supervision

questions.explanation: Self defence trainer ka primary focus students ko danger zones detect karna, physical protection methods clear karna aur escape protocols seekhana hai.

questions.question: Self defence terminology me 'Situational Awareness' (sthiti ka aakalan) kya represent karta hai?

options.A: Apne surrounding environment, people behavior aur potential threat areas ko continuously scan aur observe karna
options.B: Focusing strictly on your mobile screen while walking in dark streets
options.C: Talking loudly to show power in unknown places
options.D: Zero environmental awareness

questions.explanation: Situational awareness safety ka first step hai. Isse threat early detect ho jata hai, allowing time for verbal de-escalation or escape.

questions.question: Stand-up striking me straight punch (eg Jab/Cross) pull back (guard position) ka basic rule?

options.A: Retract punching hand back to face cheek immediately to maintain guard defence
options.B: Drop punching hand to waist level after hit
options.C: Keep arm extended straight in air permanently
options.D: Guards are not used in striking

questions.explanation: Punch hit hone ke baad instant chin protection guard position par hand return hona defence parameters maintain karta hai.

questions.question: Self defence scenarios me front attack grip 'Lapel Grab' escape check, what is the best initial action?

options.A: Control attacker hands, secure balance base, strike sensitive zone (eg throat/groin) and rotate/break grip
options.B: Pull back forcefully in straight line behind attacker strength
options.C: Cry and yield balance without defense movements
options.D: Forceful pulls are the only escapes

questions.explanation: Straight pulls attacker grip ko aur tight karti hain. weight control, redirection aur strike leverage easily grip open karte hain.

questions.question: Verbal self defence me 'De-escalation' (mamle ko shant karna) techniques standard guideline?

options.A: Keep voice calm/assertive, maintain safe distance, use non-threatening open hand gestures, and offer escape verbal routes
options.B: Screaming insults back to show strength limits
options.C: Staring aggressively into attacker eyes holding fists up
options.D: Zero verbal negotiation applies

questions.explanation: De-escalation targets preventing physical fights. Open hands show non-aggression while keeping defense blocks ready.

questions.question: Martial arts safety checks me 'Sparring' (dono students ki practice fight) supervision standard rules?

options.A: Mandatory safety gear (mouthguard, headgear, shin guards, gloves), strict intensity control, and immediate coach stop whistles
options.B: Allow students to hit with maximum force without gloves
options.C: Leave students alone in room to spar freely
options.D: Safety gears are ignored in sparring tests

questions.explanation: Sparring drills injury prevention guidelines par depend karti hain. Mouthguards teeth aur brain trauma shock absorb karte hain.

questions.question: Self defence mechanics me body weight falls checks 'Breakfalls' (Ukemi) why are they trained?

options.A: Distribute fall impact energy across wide surface area, keeping head and joints safe from crash impact
options.B: Ensure body lands flat with straight locked hands on floor
options.C: Breakfall drills are only for gymnastic shows
options.D: Zero fall protection exists

questions.explanation: Breakfalls absorb shock using slaps and tucking the chin, avoiding concussions and wrist fractures.

questions.question: Close quarter combat checks me 'Vulnerable targets' (najuk ang) focus order in real threat scenarios?

options.A: Eyes, Nose, Throat, Groin, and Knees (targets that require minimal force to disable threats)
options.B: Heavy chest muscles and shoulder bones
options.C: Attacking attacker shoes and belt loops
options.D: Vulnerable targets are avoided in self-defense

questions.explanation: Groin/eyes hits threat visual/mobility controls check instantly damage karti hain, allowing victim to escape safely.

questions.question: Self defence legal limits me 'Reasonable Force' principle kya state karta hai?

options.A: Force used in self-defence must match threat level; stop applying force once threat is disabled or escape is clear
options.B: Eliminate attacker completely even after they surrender
options.C: Force is only legal if you hit first in public
options.D: Legal rules do not apply in self defense conflicts

questions.explanation: Excessive force post threat neutralization changes legal status from victim to attacker under criminal codes.

questions.question: Martial arts dojo/dojang management ethics checks me instructor guidelines?

options.A: Promote discipline, respect, safety, self-control and zero tolerance for bullying or street fight misuse
options.B: Allow senior students to bully junior newcomers to test mental toughness
options.C: Teach lethal choke techniques to children classes
options.D: Zero ethical codes apply

questions.explanation: Dojo ethics focus on respect and discipline (eg bowing, control), keeping martial arts training positive.

questions.question: Stand-up striking 'Elbow strike' why is it effective in close-range self-defence?

options.A: Elbow bone is extremely hard and sharp, creating high impact force without risk of breaking own hand bones
options.B: Elbow strikes require long wind-up runs
options.C: Elbow strikes are slow and easy to block
options.D: Elbow strikes have zero impact force

questions.explanation: Elbows are hard, sharp bones. They are highly effective in close-range self-defence, avoiding hand injuries.

questions.question: Grappling defense 'Sprawl' when is this technique used?

options.A: Defending against double-leg or single-leg takedown attempts (driving hips back and down to floor)
options.B: Escaping from a rear choke hold
options.C: Striking with high roundhouse kicks
options.D: Sprawls are used only in boxing matches

questions.explanation: Sprawling drops your weight onto the attacker's head/neck, neutralizing their tackle attempt.

questions.question: Self-defence concepts 'OODA Loop' what does O-O-D-A stand for?

options.A: Observe, Orient, Decide, Act (decision-making loop for high-stress conflicts)
options.B: Overcome, Organize, Defend, Attack
options.C: Obtain, Order, Deliver, Alert
options.D: OODA is not a defense term

questions.explanation: The OODA loop models fast tactical decision-making, helping you process and act under pressure.

questions.question: Self-defence tactics 'The Boundary Line' what safe distance is recommended against unarmed threat?

options.A: Two arms length (reaction gap zone; gives time to react if attacker lunges)
options.B: Within touching distance (elbow range)
options.C: 10 meters away always
options.D: Safe distance is not monitored

questions.explanation: A two-arm reaction gap gives you time to block or dodge if a threat lunges.

questions.question: Dojo safety operations 'First Aid for nosebleed' what is correct sequence?

options.A: Pinch soft part of nose, lean head slightly forward, and breathe through mouth (hold for 10 minutes)
options.B: Tilt head backward and plug nose with tissue paper
options.C: Force student to lie flat on back and apply hot compress
options.D: Nosebleeds do not require first aid steps

questions.explanation: Leaning forward prevents blood from draining down the throat, which can cause gagging and nausea.

options.C: 10 meters away always

questions.question: Nosebleed first aid lean forward pinch?

options.A: Pinch soft part of nose, lean head slightly forward, and breathe through mouth (hold for 10 minutes)
options.B: Tilt head backward and plug nose with tissue paper
options.C: Force student to lie flat on back and apply hot compress
options.D: Nosebleeds do not require first aid steps

questions.explanation: Leaning forward prevents blood from draining down the throat, which can cause gagging and nausea.

questions.question: Stand-up striking mechanics 'Front Kick' what is target landing surface of foot?

options.A: Ball of foot (extending ankle and flexing toes back to avoid toe fractures)
options.B: Flat heel surface only
options.C: Tips of toes to stretch reach
options.D: Landing surface does not affect foot safety

questions.explanation: Kicking with the ball of the foot focuses force while pulling toes back prevents painful joint fractures.

questions.question: Grappling defense 'The Guard' what does it define in ground combat?

options.A: Defensive position on back using legs to control and redirect attacker weight/movements
options.B: Running away from ground fights completely
options.C: Holding fists in front of face while standing
options.D: Guard is not a ground position

questions.explanation: The guard is a core BJJ position. It uses the legs to defend, sweep, or submit an attacker from your back.

questions.question: Self-defence psychology 'The Color Code of Awareness' (Jeff Cooper) what does Condition Yellow indicate?

options.A: Relaxed alertness (non-specific scan of surroundings; standard state when in public)
options.B: Completely relaxed and unaware (Condition White)
options.C: Target identified and ready to fight (Condition Orange)
options.D: Active combat state (Condition Red)

questions.explanation: Condition Yellow is relaxed alertness. You scan your surroundings without panic, avoiding surprise attacks.

questions.question: Dojo safety operations 'Sparring intensity rules' what does light contact indicate?

options.A: Controlled touches focusing on form and speed (no intent to damage or knock out)
options.B: Using 50% force and heavy punches
options.C: Sparring without safety pads or gears
options.D: Light contact has zero intensity limits

questions.explanation: Light contact sparring builds reflexes and form safely, preventing concussions and severe injuries.

questions.question: Instructor ethics checks 'Promoting street fights' what is policy for students who misuse techniques?

options.A: Immediate suspension or expulsion from dojo to preserve martial arts ethics standards
options.B: Praise student publicly to encourage self confidence
options.C: Offer higher belt ranks as promotion rewards
options.D: Misuse of techniques is not monitored

questions.explanation: Expelling violators protects dojo ethics, reminding students that self-defence skills are for protection, not aggression.

questions.question: Front kick ball of foot toes?

options.A: Ball of foot (extending ankle and flexing toes back to avoid toe fractures)
options.B: Flat heel surface only
options.C: Tips of toes to stretch reach
options.D: Landing surface does not affect foot safety

questions.explanation: Kicking with the ball of the foot focuses force while pulling toes back prevents painful joint fractures.

options.D: Guard is not a ground position

questions.explanation: The guard is a core BJJ position. It uses the legs to defend, sweep, or submit an attacker from your back.

options.B: Completely relaxed and unaware (Condition White)
options.C: Target identified and ready to fight (Condition Orange)
options.D: Active combat state (Condition Red)

questions.explanation: Condition Yellow is relaxed alertness. You scan your surroundings without panic, avoiding surprise attacks.

questions.question: Sparring light contact speed form touch?

options.A: Controlled touches focusing on form and speed (no intent to damage or knock out)
options.B: Using 50% force and heavy punches
options.C: Sparring without safety pads or gears
options.D: Light contact has zero intensity limits

questions.explanation: Light contact sparring builds reflexes and form safely, preventing concussions and severe injuries.

questions.question: Misuse of martial arts street fights expulsion?

options.A: Immediate suspension or expulsion from dojo to preserve martial arts ethics standards
options.B: Praise student publicly to encourage self confidence
options.C: Offer higher belt ranks as promotion rewards
options.D: Misuse of techniques is not monitored

questions.explanation: Expelling violators protects dojo ethics, reminding students that self-defence skills are for protection, not aggression.

questions.question: Stand-up striking 'Knee strike' what is correct hip drive technique?

options.A: Drive hips forward during strike, pulling opponent into knee to maximize impact force
options.B: Keep hips tucked back while raising knee upwards
options.C: Perform knee strike while jumping backward
options.D: Knee strikes do not use hip drive

questions.explanation: Driving your hips forward adds core power to knee strikes, making them effective close-range moves.

questions.question: Grappling defense 'The Guillotine choke escape' what is first action step?

options.A: Secure attacker hand to prevent choke tightening, clear head over shoulder, and pass guard
options.B: Pull head back forcefully with all strength
options.C: Slam own back to floor immediately
options.D: Guillotine escapes are not possible

questions.explanation: Securing the choking hand prevents choking pressure, allowing you to pass guard and free your head.

questions.question: Self-defence concepts 'Reaction time gap' why is it critical against weapon threats?

options.A: Unarmed defender reacts slower than attacker action; keep safe distance to compensate for reaction lag
options.B: Action is slower than reaction in combat
options.C: Weapon threats eliminate reaction time gaps
options.D: Reaction time is identical for all actions

questions.explanation: Action is faster than reaction. Maintaining distance compensates for reaction lag, helping you dodge or block weapon strikes.

questions.question: Dojo safety logs 'Mouthguard requirements' why are they mandatory in sparring?

options.A: Protects teeth from fracture, prevents lip lacerations, and reduces concussion risks by cushioning jaw impact
options.B: Mouthguards improve breathing capacity of students
options.C: Required by chemical smell regulations
options.D: Mouthguards are optional safety items

questions.explanation: Mouthguards cushion jaw impacts, protecting teeth and lowering the risk of concussions.

questions.question: Dojo management 'Belt testing audits' what is verified by examiner?

options.A: Technical execution, sparring control, code of conduct, and attendance hours
options.B: Student's personal fee records only
options.C: Strength score on weight machines
options.D: Belt tests do not require evaluations

questions.explanation: Belt testing reviews form, sparring control, and discipline, ensuring students are ready for higher ranks.

questions.question: Knee strike hip drive forward pull?

options.A: Drive hips forward during strike, pulling opponent into knee to maximize impact force
options.B: Keep hips tucked back while raising knee upwards
options.C: Perform knee strike while jumping backward
options.D: Knee strikes do not use hip drive

questions.explanation: Driving your hips forward adds core power to knee strikes, making them effective close-range moves.

questions.question: Guillotine choke hand control guard pass?

options.A: Secure attacker hand to prevent choke tightening, clear head over shoulder, and pass guard
options.B: Pull head back forcefully with all strength
options.C: Slam own back to floor immediately
options.D: Guillotine escapes are not possible

questions.explanation: Securing the choking hand prevents choking pressure, allowing you to pass guard and free your head.

options.B: Action is slower than reaction in combat
options.C: Weapon threats eliminate reaction time gaps
options.D: Reaction time is identical for all actions

questions.explanation: Action is faster than reaction. Maintaining distance compensates for reaction lag, helping you dodge or block weapon strikes.

questions.question: Mouthguard teeth protection jaw cushion concussion?

options.B: Mouthguards improve breathing capacity of students
options.C: Required by chemical smell regulations
options.D: Mouthguards are optional safety items

questions.explanation: Mouthguards cushion jaw impacts, protecting teeth and lowering the risk of concussions.

questions.question: Belt testing technical form sparring control?

options.A: Technical execution, sparring control, code of conduct, and attendance hours
options.B: Student's personal fee records only
options.C: Strength score on weight machines
options.D: Belt tests do not require evaluations

questions.explanation: Belt testing reviews form, sparring control, and discipline, ensuring students are ready for higher ranks.

questions.question: Striking techniques 'Low Roundhouse Kick' what is target landing surface of leg?

options.A: Lower part of shinbone (tibia) to avoid breaking small foot bones
options.B: Tips of toes to extend reach
options.C: Flat heel surface only
options.D: Landing surface does not affect roundhouse safety

questions.explanation: Striking with the shinbone provides a hard surface, avoiding fractures in the foot bones during roundhouse kicks.

questions.question: Grappling defense 'The Rear Naked Choke escape' what is first action step?

options.A: Tuck chin tightly into neck, pull down choking arm using two-on-one hand grip, and turn into the choke
options.B: Slam back to floor aggressively
options.C: Pull attacker hair behind head
options.D: Choke escapes are not possible from behind

questions.explanation: Tucking your chin and pulling the arm down relieves airway pressure, letting you turn and escape.

questions.question: Self-defence concepts 'The Tueller Drill' (21-foot rule) what does it demonstrate?

options.A: An attacker with a knife can cover 21 feet before an officer can draw and fire a handgun
options.B: Safe distance to run from punches
options.C: Time taken to execute roundhouse kicks
options.D: Drill has zero self-defence value

questions.explanation: The Tueller Drill highlights that a knife threat within 21 feet is dangerous, demanding quick reaction and movement.

questions.question: Dojo safety operations 'Joint lock submissions' when should student tap out?

options.A: Immediately when lock is secured and before pain starts (tap early to avoid joint injury)
options.B: Only when joint bones start cracking
options.C: Wait for supervisor to whistle stop
options.D: Tapping out is banned in dojo rules

questions.explanation: Tapping early prevents hyperextension injuries, ensuring safe and sustainable joint sparring.

questions.question: Dojo branding ethics 'Teaching lethal moves' what is standard restriction?

options.A: Restrict lethal or high-risk moves (eg windpipe strikes, eye gouges) to advanced adult classes; emphasize escape first for beginners
options.B: Teach lethal strikes to young children in first lesson
options.C: Teach lethal moves to anyone without restriction
options.D: Dojos do not teach self-defence moves

questions.explanation: Limiting high-risk moves to advanced classes ensures students have the maturity and control to practice them safely.

questions.question: Low roundhouse shinbone tibia landing?

options.A: Lower part of shinbone (tibia) to avoid breaking small foot bones
options.B: Tips of toes to extend reach
options.C: Flat heel surface only
options.D: Landing surface does not affect roundhouse safety

questions.explanation: Striking with the shinbone provides a hard surface, avoiding fractures in the foot bones during roundhouse kicks.

questions.question: Rear naked choke tuck chin pull?

options.A: Tuck chin tightly into neck, pull down choking arm using two-on-one hand grip, and turn into the choke
options.B: Slam back to floor aggressively
options.C: Pull attacker hair behind head
options.D: Choke escapes are not possible from behind

questions.explanation: Tucking your chin and pulling the arm down relieves airway pressure, letting you turn and escape.

questions.question: Tueller Drill 21 foot knife draw?

options.A: An attacker with a knife can cover 21 feet before an officer can draw and fire a handgun
options.B: Safe distance to run from punches
options.C: Time taken to execute roundhouse kicks
options.D: Drill has zero self-defence value

questions.explanation: The Tueller Drill highlights that a knife threat within 21 feet is dangerous, demanding quick reaction and movement.

questions.question: Joint lock tap early pain avoid?

options.A: Immediately when lock is secured and before pain starts (tap early to avoid joint injury)
options.B: Only when joint bones start cracking
options.C: Wait for supervisor to whistle stop
options.D: Tapping out is banned in dojo rules

questions.explanation: Tapping early prevents hyperextension injuries, ensuring safe and sustainable joint sparring.

questions.question: Restrict lethal windpipe advanced class?

options.A: Restrict lethal or high-risk moves (eg windpipe strikes, eye gouges) to advanced adult classes; emphasize escape first for beginners
options.B: Teach lethal strikes to young children in first lesson
options.C: Teach lethal moves to anyone without restriction
options.D: Dojos do not teach self-defence moves

questions.explanation: Limiting high-risk moves to advanced classes ensures students have the maturity and control to practice them safely.

questions.question: Striking techniques 'Hammer Fist' what is target landing surface of hand?

options.A: Fleshy bottom edge of fist (hypothenar eminence), like using a hammer
options.B: Top knuckles of index and middle fingers
options.C: Tips of fingers (spear hand)
options.D: Hammer fist uses open palm slap

questions.explanation: The hammer fist uses the fleshy side of the fist, protecting hand bones while delivering high-impact force.

questions.question: Grappling defense 'The Mount Escape' (Upa/Bridge) what is first action step?

options.A: Trap attacker arm and foot on same side, bridge hips up aggressively, and roll over trapped side
options.B: Push attacker chest with straight arms
options.C: Pull attacker head down into guard
options.D: Mount escapes do not use hip bridges

questions.explanation: Trapping the arm/leg on one side prevents the attacker from bracing, enabling you to roll them over with a hip bridge.

questions.question: Self-defence concepts 'The Under-the-jaw push' when is it used?

options.A: To create space and drive attacker head back when grabbed from front (head follows chin direction)
options.B: To lock attacker shoulder joint from behind
options.C: To throw attacker over hip line
options.D: Pushing jaw is not used in combat

questions.explanation: Pushing the chin up and back forces the head back, breaking the attacker's balance and creating space to escape.

questions.question: Dojo safety operations 'Tatami mats cleanliness' how is ringworm prevented?

options.A: Sanitize mats daily with antifungal disinfectant cleaner, and require clean feet/no shoes on mat
options.B: Wash mats with tap water once a month
options.C: Mats do not require chemical sanitization
options.D: Shoes are recommended during mat workouts

questions.explanation: Daily sanitizing with antifungal cleaners prevents the spread of skin infections like ringworm in barefoot training zones.

questions.question: Dojo business operations 'Student class waivers' why are liability release logs signed?

options.A: Verify student health status, detail sparring risks, and protect school from injury lawsuits
options.B: Required by regional transport laws
options.C: Required by Google AdSense algorithms
options.D: Waivers do not have legal utility

questions.explanation: Liability waivers document sparring risks and verify student consent, protecting the school from lawsuits.

questions.question: Hammer Fist fleshy bottom edge fist?

options.A: Fleshy bottom edge of fist (hypothenar eminence), like using a hammer
options.B: Top knuckles of index and middle fingers
options.C: Tips of fingers (spear hand)
options.D: Hammer fist uses open palm slap

questions.explanation: The hammer fist uses the fleshy side of the fist, protecting hand bones while delivering high-impact force.

questions.question: Mount escape trap arm foot bridge?

options.A: Trap attacker arm and foot on same side, bridge hips up aggressively, and roll over trapped side
options.B: Push attacker chest with straight arms
options.C: Pull attacker head down into guard
options.D: Mount escapes do not use hip bridges

questions.explanation: Trapping the arm/leg on one side prevents the attacker from bracing, enabling you to roll them over with a hip bridge.

questions.question: Under jaw chin push balance break?

options.A: To create space and drive attacker head back when grabbed from front (head follows chin direction)
options.B: To lock attacker shoulder joint from behind
options.C: To throw attacker over hip line
options.D: Pushing jaw is not used in combat

questions.explanation: Pushing the chin up and back forces the head back, breaking the attacker's balance and creating space to escape.

questions.question: Antifungal mat sanitize ringworm prevention?

options.A: Sanitize mats daily with antifungal disinfectant cleaner, and require clean feet/no shoes on mat
options.B: Wash mats with tap water once a month
options.C: Mats do not require chemical sanitization
options.D: Shoes are recommended during mat workouts

questions.explanation: Daily sanitizing with antifungal cleaners prevents the spread of skin infections like ringworm in barefoot training zones.

questions.question: Liability waivers dojo injury protection?

options.A: Verify student health status, detail sparring risks, and protect school from injury lawsuits
options.B: Required by regional transport laws
options.C: Required by Google AdSense algorithms
options.D: Waivers do not have legal utility

questions.explanation: Liability waivers document sparring risks and verify student consent, protecting the school from lawsuits.

questions.question: Striking mechanics 'The Palm Strike' why is it preferred over closed-fist punch in self-defence?

options.A: Palms offer large landing surface and protect small knuckle bones from breaking on hard targets (eg skull)
options.B: Palm strikes carry double energy automatically
options.C: Palm strikes can only target shoulder blades
options.D: Closed-fist punches have zero injury risk

questions.explanation: Palm strikes hit with the heel of the hand, avoiding the knuckle fractures common in closed-fist strikes to the skull.

questions.question: Grappling defense 'The Side Control Escape' (Underhook and Hip Heist) what is key transition?

options.A: Frame chest, bridge/shrimp to create space, secure underhook, and transition to hands/knees
options.B: Pull attacker neck down into headlock
options.C: Slam back to floor and wait for reset
options.D: Side control escapes are not taught

questions.explanation: Framing and shrimping creates the space needed to slip an underhook in, helping you escape side control.

questions.question: Self-defence tactics 'The Groin Strike' why is it targeted in street defence?

options.A: Groin is highly sensitive to pain; strike disables mobility and breaks attacker focus instantly, allowing escape
options.B: Groin strikes require complex spinning mechanics
options.C: Groin strikes are strictly illegal in street self-defence situations
options.D: Zero tactical benefit exists

questions.explanation: A groin strike causes immediate pain and breaks the attacker's focus, creating a clear window to escape.

questions.question: Dojo safety operations 'Handling joint hyperextensions' what is immediate first aid?

options.A: Immobilize joint, apply cold ice wrap, elevate limb, and seek medical review (do not pull/twist joint)
options.B: Force student to perform flex stretching exercises
options.C: Apply hot pads directly to swollen joint
options.D: Hyperextended joints require zero first aid

questions.explanation: Immobilizing and icing a hyperextended joint limits swelling and protects ligaments from further strain.

questions.question: Instructor professional ethics 'Safety during sparring demonstrations' what is the rule?

options.A: Instructors must demonstrate techniques with controlled speed and force, prioritizing student safety
options.B: Instructors should use full force to demonstrate authority
options.C: Sparring demos are banned in dojos
options.D: Safety rules do not apply to instructors

questions.explanation: Demonstrating with control shows proper form without injury risks, modeling professional behavior for students.

questions.question: Striking techniques 'The Hook Punch' what is correct arm angle?

options.A: 90-degree bend at elbow (arm parallel to floor, rotating entire torso to drive power)
options.B: Arm straight at elbow
options.C: Wrist flexed forward at 45 degrees
options.D: Hook punch has zero elbow angle requirements

questions.explanation: Keeping a 90-degree elbow bend and rotating your core drives power through hook punches safely.

questions.question: Grappling defense 'The Choke Release' (Guillotine) what is correct head movement direction?

options.A: Turn head into attacker's ribs and pass guard laterally (avoid pulling head straight out)
options.B: Pull head straight back forcefully
options.C: Slam own forehead to floor
options.D: Guillotine chokes have no head release paths

questions.explanation: Turning into the ribs relieves airway pressure, letting you pass guard and escape.

questions.question: Self-defence concepts 'The Solar Plexus strike' why is it targeted?

options.A: Center chest nerve cluster; strike interrupts breathing diaphragm, causing temporary wind knockout
options.B: Fractions ribs instantly with light touch
options.C: Solar plexus strikes cause blindness
options.D: Zero physiological effect exists

questions.explanation: A strike to the solar plexus disrupts the diaphragm, knocking the wind out of an attacker and creating an escape window.

questions.question: Dojo safety operations 'Mats sanitization' what is standard disinfectant target?

options.A: Virucidal, bactericidal, and fungicidal spray (prevents ringworm, staph, and herpes gladiatorum)
options.B: Clear tap water without chemical soap
options.C: Mild perfumed spray deodorants only
options.D: Mats do not require disinfectants

questions.explanation: Broad-spectrum disinfectants destroy skin pathogens (ringworm, staph), keeping bare-foot zones clean.

options.B: Only encouraging students to start street fights for practice
options.C: Operating heavy weapons and artillery equipment
options.D: Zero class supervision

questions.question: Self defence situational awareness environmental scanning?

options.A: Apne surrounding environment, people behavior aur potential threat areas ko continuously scan aur observe karna
options.B: Focusing strictly on your mobile screen while walking in dark streets
options.C: Talking loudly to show power in unknown places
options.D: Zero environmental awareness

questions.explanation: Situational awareness safety ka first step hai. Isse threat early detect ho jata hai, allowing time for verbal de-escalation or escape.

questions.question: Straight punch guard faces cheek pull?

options.A: Retract punching hand back to face cheek immediately to maintain guard defence
options.B: Drop punching hand to waist level after hit
options.C: Keep arm extended straight in air permanently
options.D: Guards are not used in striking

questions.explanation: Punch hit hone ke baad instant chin protection guard position par hand return hona defence parameters maintain karta hai.

questions.question: Lapel grab escape balance strike groin?

options.A: Control attacker hands, secure balance base, strike sensitive zone (eg throat/groin) and rotate/break grip
options.B: Pull back forcefully in straight line behind attacker strength
options.C: Cry and yield balance without defense movements
options.D: Forceful pulls are the only escapes

questions.explanation: Straight pulls attacker grip ko aur tight karti hain. weight control, redirection aur strike leverage easily grip open karte hain.

questions.question: Verbal de-escalation open hand gestures conflict?

options.A: Keep voice calm/assertive, maintain safe distance, use non-threatening open hand gestures, and offer escape verbal routes
options.B: Screaming insults back to show strength limits
options.C: Staring aggressively into attacker eyes holding fists up
options.D: Zero verbal negotiation applies

questions.explanation: De-escalation targets preventing physical fights. Open hands show non-aggression while keeping defense blocks ready.

questions.question: Sparring safety equipment mouthguard stop whistles?

options.A: Mandatory safety gear (mouthguard, headgear, shin guards, gloves), strict intensity control, and immediate coach stop whistles
options.B: Allow students to hit with maximum force without gloves
options.C: Leave students alone in room to spar freely
options.D: Safety gears are ignored in sparring tests

questions.explanation: Sparring drills injury prevention guidelines par depend karti hain. Mouthguards teeth aur brain trauma shock absorb karte hain.

questions.question: Ukemi breakfall shock absorption head protection?

options.A: Distribute fall impact energy across wide surface area, keeping head and joints safe from crash impact
options.B: Ensure body lands flat with straight locked hands on floor
options.C: Breakfall drills are only for gymnastic shows
options.D: Zero fall protection exists

questions.explanation: Breakfalls absorb shock using slaps and tucking the chin, avoiding concussions and wrist fractures.

questions.question: Real combat vulnerable targets eyes groin throat?

options.A: Eyes, Nose, Throat, Groin, and Knees (targets that require minimal force to disable threats)
options.B: Heavy chest muscles and shoulder bones
options.C: Attacking attacker shoes and belt loops
options.D: Vulnerable targets are avoided in self-defense

questions.explanation: Groin/eyes hits threat visual/mobility controls check instantly damage karti hain, allowing victim to escape safely.

questions.question: Reasonable Force threat disabled criminal codes?

options.A: Force used in self-defence must match threat level; stop applying force once threat is disabled or escape is clear
options.B: Eliminate attacker completely even after they surrender
options.C: Force is only legal if you hit first in public
options.D: Legal rules do not apply in self defense conflicts

questions.explanation: Excessive force post threat neutralization changes legal status from victim to attacker under criminal codes.
